name: Carl Icahn
notes: Classic corporate raider turned activist. Targets underperforming companies for board seats, asset sales, buybacks. Track 13D filings via Icahn Enterprises.

name: Bill Ackman
notes: Pershing Square. 5-8 concentrated positions, activist or friendly. Mix of value and growth-at-reasonable-price. Also files 13F quarterly.

name: Dan Loeb
notes: Third Point. Known for poison-pen letters demanding change. Mix of activist, event-driven, distressed.

name: Paul Singer
notes: Elliott Management. Aggressive activist, often forces M&A, board changes, operational restructuring. Large positions. Also in distressed/sovereign debt.

name: Nelson Peltz
notes: Trian Partners. Focuses on operational improvements at large-caps (P&G, Disney, Unilever). Board seats, not hostile takeovers.

name: Jeff Smith
notes: Starboard Value. Mid/large-cap operational activist. Board representation, cost cuts, margin improvement. ~100 campaigns.

name: Barry Rosenstein
notes: JANA Partners. 'Constructivist' — typically works behind scenes. Targets conglomerates for breakups. Also ESG activism.

name: Jeff Ubben / Mason Morfit
notes: ValueAct Capital. Friendly activist — takes board seats by invitation. Long holding periods. Tech, healthcare.

name: Warren Buffett
notes: Berkshire Hathaway. Rarely files 13D (mostly 13G passive), but a 13D from Berkshire is a massive signal. Cross-ref with 13F.

name: Michael Burry
notes: Scion Asset Management. Deep value, contrarian. CAUTION: heavy options/puts usage — a 13F put position is bearish, not bullish.
